title: cli commands.rs: incoming ClearMessage::Chat text written to history without size limit
description: cli/src/commands.rs DELIVER handler: for ClearMessage::Chat, history.append_received(&from, text.as_bytes()) is called unconditionally. There is no upper bound on text length. An MLS peer can send a multi-megabyte text field through the relay and the CLI will write it all to the SQLite history DB. Combined with the existing nie-f0pz.9 finding (no size limit on /command output before MLS encryption), this means an attacker inside the MLS group can fill the history DB disk. The outbound direction has a MAX_TEXT_BYTES=65536 check in daemon/api.rs but the CLI receive path has none.
